classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

title: Canon I.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: The holy synod assembled in Sardica from various provinces decreed as follows.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: (Found in Greek in John of Constantinople’s collection of the sixth century and several other mss.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Found also in the works of the Greek scholiasts.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Found in Latin in the Prisca, in Dionysius Exiguus, and in Isidore, genuine and false.)

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Hosius, bishop of the city of Corduba, said: A prevalent evil, or rather most mischievous corruption must be done away with from its very foundations.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Let no bishop be allowed to remove from a small city to a different one: as there is an obvious reason for this fault, accounting for such attempts; since no bishop could ever yet be found who endeavoured to be translated from a larger city to a smaller one.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: It is therefore evident that such persons are inflamed with excessive covetousness and are only serving ambition in order to have the repute of possessing greater authority.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Is it then the pleasure of all that so grave an abuse be punished with great severity?

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: For I think that men of this sort should not be admitted even to lay communion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: All the bishops said: It is the pleasure of all.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Bishop Hosius said: A prevalent evil and mischievous corruption must be done away with from its foundation.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Let no bishop be allowed to remove from his own city to another.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: For the reason of such attempts is manifest, since in this matter no bishop has been found who would remove from a larger city to a smaller one.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: It is therefore evident that these men are inflamed with excess of covetousness, and are serving ambition and aiming at the possession of power.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: If it be the pleasure of all, let so great an evil be punished right harshly and sternly, so that he who is such shall not even be admitted to lay communion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: All with one accord answered: Such is our pleasure.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon I.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: No bishop is to be found passing from a smaller to a greater city.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: If anyone should move from an humble to a more important see, he shall be excommunicated through his whole life as proud and grasping.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: (Dissert. in Synod.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Sard., § II.)

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: What Peter de Marca says (De Concordia Sacerdotii et Imp., Lib.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: V., cap. iv.), “Hosius presided over” this council as legate of the Roman bishop, rests upon no solid foundation, and no trace of any such legation is found in Athanasius or in any of the other writers who treated of this synod.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: Moreover such a thing is contrary to the form of subscription used.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: For of those who signed the first is Hosius, and Athanasius designates him simply as “from Spain,” without any addition; and then next he mentions “Julius of Rome, by Archidamus and Philoxenus, his presbyters,” etc. What is clearer than that, by the testimony of Athanasius, Julius was present by these two presbyters only, and that they only were his legates or vicars, who in his room were present at this synod?

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Sardica.
verses.text: The first part of this canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici; Raymund’s Decretales, De Clericis non residentibus, Cap. ii.
